---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Elizabet Sanyal"
date: "2023-06-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(reticulate)
library(tensorflow)
library(abind)
library(keras)
library(xts)
library(PerformanceAnalytics)
library(kableExtra)
library(lubridate)
library(tidyverse)
library(purrr)
load("~/R/Test_GPU/data_ml.RData")
stock_ids <- levels(as.factor(data_ml$stock_id)) # A list of all stock_ids
stock_days <- data_ml %>%                        # Compute the number of data points per stock
    group_by(stock_id) %>% summarize(nb = n()) 
stock_ids_short <- stock_ids[which(stock_days$nb == max(stock_days$nb))] # Stocks with full data
returns <- data_ml %>%                           # Compute returns, in matrix format, in 3 steps:
    filter(stock_id %in% stock_ids_short) %>%    # 1. Filtering the data
    dplyr::select(date, stock_id, R1M_Usd) %>%   # 2. Keep returns along with dates & firm names
    spread(key = stock_id, value = R1M_Usd)      # 3. Put in matrix shape 
features <- colnames(data_ml[3:95]) # Keep the feature's column names (hard-coded, beware!)
features_short <- c("Div_Yld", "Eps", "Mkt_Cap_12M_Usd", "Mom_11M_Usd", 
                    "Ocf", "Pb", "Vol1Y_Usd", "Debtequity", "Cash_Per_Share", "Asset_Turnover")
data_ml <- data_ml %>% 
    group_by(date) %>%                                   # Group by date
    mutate(R1M_Usd_C = R1M_Usd > median(R1M_Usd),        # Create the categorical labels
           R12M_Usd_C = R1M_Usd > median(R12M_Usd)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate_if(is.logical, as.factor)




data_short <- data_ml %>%         # Shorter dataset
    filter(stock_id %in% stock_ids_short) %>%
    dplyr::select(c("stock_id", "date",features_short, "R1M_Usd"))
dates <- unique(data_short$date)  # Vector of dates for the training period

glimpse(data_short)

```


```{r}

data_wide <- data_short %>%  select(-R1M_Usd)%>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = stock_id, values_from = Div_Yld:Asset_Turnover) %>% arrange(date) %>% select(-date) 

returns <- data_short %>%  select(R1M_Usd,stock_id, date) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = stock_id, values_from = R1M_Usd) %>% arrange(date) %>% select(-date)

```



```{r}

sharpe_ratio_loss <- function(y_ret, y_pred) {
  backend <-  backend()
  # Multiply predicted weights by observed returns
  
  weighted_returns <-  y_ret*y_pred
  portfolio_return <- k_sum(weighted_returns, axis = 2)

 #k_print_tensor(portfolio_return, "That returns of the batch portfolios")
 
  # Calculate mean and standard deviation of portfolio return
  mean_return <- backend$mean(portfolio_return)

 
  std_return <- backend$std(portfolio_return)
 
  # Calculate Sharpe ratio
  sharpe_ratio <- (mean_returnbackend$constant(0.001)) / (std_return + backend$constant(0.001))
  # Minimize the negative Sharpe ratio (maximize the Sharpe ratio)
  return(-sharpe_ratio)
}

# Custom activation function
my_activation <- function(x) {
   
backend <-  backend()
constant_two <- (k_sum(x, axis = 2, keepdims = TRUE)+backend$epsilon())

  output <- tf$divide(x, constant_two)

  return(output)
}


```


```{r}
rate <- 0.5
units_lin <-  481
units_nlin_i <- 321
activation_nl_i <- "tanh"
units_conc <- 1
activation_conc <- "swish"
learning_rate <-0.01
units_nlin_ii <- 385
activation_nl_ii <-"swish"


build_model <- function(rate, units_lin,units_nlin_i, activation_nl_i,units_conc,activation_conc,learning_rate,units_nlin_ii, activation_nl_ii ) {
    layer_1 <- layer_input(shape = c(num_features), 
                           name = "Linear_Model_Input")
    
    layer_linear <-  layer_1 %>% 
                           layer_dense(units = units_lin, 
                                       activation = "linear",
                                       kernel_initializer = initializer_random_normal(mean = 0.05, stddev = 0.05)
                                       ) %>%
                           layer_dropout(rate = rate) 
    
     layer_2 <- layer_input(shape = c(num_features), name = "Non-Linear_Model_Input")
     
     layer_nonlinear <- layer_2 %>% layer_dense(units = units_nlin_i,
                                                activation = activation_nl_i,
                                                kernel_initializer = initializer_random_normal(mean = 0.05, stddev = 0.05)
                                                )  %>% layer_dropout(rate = rate)
       
      # layer_nonlinear <- layer_nonlinear %>%  layer_dense(units = units_nlin_ii,                                                                              #            activation = activation_nl_ii,kernel_initializer = initializer_random_normal(mean = 0.05, stddev = 0.05))

      concat <- layer_concatenate(list(layer_linear, layer_nonlinear))
      layer_3 <- concat %>%
                    layer_dense(units = units_conc, 
                                activation = activation_conc, 
                                kernel_initializer = initializer_random_normal(mean = 0.05, stddev = 0.05))

# Define the rest of the model
        output <- layer_3 %>%
                 layer_dense(units = length(stock_ids_short), 
                             activation = "linear",
                             kernel_initializer = initializer_random_normal(mean = 0.05, stddev = 0.05))  %>% 
          layer_lambda(my_activation) # full investment constrain

          model <- keras_model(inputs = list(layer_1, layer_2), outputs = output)

# Compile the model
           model %>% compile(
               loss = sharpe_ratio_loss,
               optimizer = optimizer_adam(learning_rate = 0.001)
                             
                             )
           return(model)
           } 
```

```{r}
# Define dropout rate

num_features <- 3720
val_window <- 50
training_window <- 60
 
backtest <- function(t, data_wide, returns) {
 
    history <- list()
    predictions <- list()
  
    window_data <- as.matrix(data_wide[1:t,])
    window_returns <- as.matrix(returns[1:t,])
    val_window_data <- as.matrix(data_wide[t:(t+val_window),])
    val_returns <- as.matrix(returns[t:(t+val_window),])
    data_predict <- as.matrix(data_wide[(t+1+val_window),])
    
    model <- build_model(rate, units_lin,units_nlin_i, activation_nl_i,units_conc,activation_conc,learning_rate,units_nlin_ii, activation_nl_ii )
    
    window_prediction <- predict(model, list(data_predict,data_predict)
                                 )
 
     early_stopping <- callback_early_stopping(monitor = "val_loss",
                                               patience = 15,
                                               restore_best_weights = TRUE
                                               )

    history[[t]] <- model %>% fit( x = list(window_data, window_data), 
                                   y = window_returns, 
                                   epochs = 60, 
                                   batch_size = 24, 
                                   verbose = 2,  
                                   validation_data = list(list(val_window_data, val_window_data), val_returns),
                                   shuffle=FALSE,
                                   callbacks = list(early_stopping))
  return(window_prediction)
}


```



```{r}


testing_periods <-  nrow(data_wide)-training_window-val_window
#max possible nrow(data_wide)-training_window-val_window

t_values <- seq(training_window, training_window+testing_periods)

backtest_results <- map(t_values, backtest, data_wide, returns)

test_sample_returns <- as.matrix(returns[(training_window+val_window+1):(training_window+val_window+testing_periods+1),])
weights_sample_returns <- do.call(rbind, lapply(backtest_results, unlist))
test_pf_ret <- rowSums(weights_sample_returns *  test_sample_returns)

#Metrics:

mean(test_pf_ret, na.rm = TRUE)/sd(test_pf_ret, na.rm = TRUE)
```
